headlines.title: NWS warns of hazardous conditions along Northern California coastline
headlines.description: The entire Northern California coastline is under beach hazard warnings. The National Weather Service warns that, for the next few days, the danger will be higher than normal. San Francisco’s Baker Beach is notorious for having exceptionally strong sneaker waves. The danger of those waves will be higher than normal, according to the NWS. Santa Cruz State Parks lifeguards said teams have been busy with water rescues for more than a week. Two Bay Area college students in their 20s were napping on a beach near Santa Cruz on June 10 when they were swept out to sea by a sneaker wave and drowned. On Thursday, a mother and a child were pulled from the water, after they were hit by a sneaker wave at Baker Beach. At last report, they were hospitalized in critical condition with non-life-threatening injuries. San Francisco Jun 18 Santa Cruz County Jun 16 Experienced Bay Area crabber Volark Keo is a moderator for the Pacifica Pier Fishing and Crabbing group. He is charge of warning crabbers about the surf dangers in social media advisories. “Basically, respect the wave. For me, I pray before during and after, and always keep my head on a swivel, no matter what. And if you have a bad feeling, get out. Crab another day. Live another day,” he said. The National Weather Service advises beach goers to stay far from the water’s edge, and off jetties, rocks and piers, and as always never turn their backs on the ocean. The NWS says the Surf Hazard Advisories will remain in effect until at least Wednesday evening.

headlines.title: Police investigate shooting that left 2 injured in San Jose
headlines.description: San Jose police said they are investigating a shooting that left two people hurt Saturday night. Police said they received a call at around 10:38 p.m. in the area of Great Oaks Parkway and Lexington Avenue regarding a person who had been shot. The caller told communications their front passenger, a man, was suffering from at least one gunshot wound. Officials said the victim was taken to the hospital with non-life-threatening injuries, and the shooting was said to have occurred somewhere else in the city. At 10:58 p.m., police said they received another call regarding an additional victim. According to authorities, this victim was in the area of Roeder Road and Azucar Road and arrived at a local hospital with at least one gunshot wound. Police added his injuries were non-life-threatening. The police department said this is an ongoing investigation.

headlines.title: CHP investigates fatal crash on Hwy. 17 in Santa Cruz County
headlines.description: An investigation is underway after a fatal crash that prompted the closure of the right lane on southbound Highway 17 in Santa Cruz County on Saturday afternoon, the California Highway Patrol said. CHP said a vehicle crashed into a tree on southbound Highway 17, near Vine Hill Road, just after noon. Authorities reported the crash ended in fatality, and advised motorists the right lane of the highway would be closed for an unknown period of time while officers investigate. CHP has not released more information on this incident.

headlines.title: Organizers to add 4th screen in San Pedro Square for World Cup watch parties
headlines.description: Watch party organizers at San Pedro Square are preparing for the upcoming USMNT match in Santa Clara on July 1. Every World Cup match, the crowds in San Pedro Square seem to get bigger and bigger. When Mexico played against South Korea Thursday night, more than 25,000 World Cup watchers packed into San Pedro Square, filling bars, restaurants, and every inch of the fan zone, where three outdoor screens are set up. When USMNT beat Australia Friday, it was another record turnout. Bettina Baros, the co-owner of the Old Wagon Saloon, opened an outdoor VIP area to manage foot traffic during the matches, offering prime seating in front of the big screens. She’s already getting calls for VIP reservations when Team USA takes the pitch in Santa Clara on July 1. “July 1, oh yeah, that’s already booked. It’s going to be packed,” she said. San Pedro Square currently has three screens set up to broadcast live matches; one on each end of San Pedro Street, and one along Sharks Way. Anticipating even bigger crowds, organizers are planning to add a fourth screen along St. James Street. “That is the goal, to have it approved and ready for next week, when Mexico plays on Wednesday,” San Jose Sports Authority Executive Director John Poch said. The Bay Area Host Committee projects tourism, jobs, and spending from World Cup fans will bring economic gains to Santa Clara County estimated between $270 million and $360 million. The city of San Jose is likely to approve the permit to add a fourth screen to San Pedro Square on Monday.

headlines.title: U.S. to play Round of 32 World Cup match in Santa Clara
headlines.description: It’s official! The United States is headed to the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in Santa Clara. Paraguay’s victory over Türkiye on Friday secured the U.S. Men’s National Team’s place in the Round of 32. The result gave the Americans enough points to remain atop Group D and advance to the knockout stage. The USMNT will play its Round of 32 match at the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium on July 1 at 5 p.m. On secondary market platforms, tickets for the match start at $3,000; however, those who don’t have tickets and want to enjoy the excitement of the World Cup can go to the multiple Fan Zones throughout the Bay Area. For details, visit our 2026 FIFA World Cup guide here.

headlines.title: San Francisco celebrates Juneteenth with annual parade
headlines.description: San Francisco held its fourth annual Juneteenth Parade and celebration on Saturday. The parade moved down Market Street, with people walking, riding on floats and cruising in classic cars. “I have lived in the Bay Area for about seven years, and I always love to participate in these kinds of things,” Jessica said. The annual parade in the city began after Juneteenth became a federal holiday in 2021, meaning this is its fourth year. Mayor Daniel Lurie was among the city officials present in the celebration. “Proud to be down here on Market Street celebrating Juneteenth our black our Black community is so critical so important to the vibrancy of San Francisco you are seeing it, it show off today,” Lurie said. “Were celebrating Juneteenth you guys we’re here for Freedom then, Freedom now, still representing our ancestors,” event producer for Juneteenth Tamara Walker said. Juneteenth commemorates the day when enslaved people in 1865 learned of their freedom, two years after the Emancipation Proclamation. Walker says it’s important for the community to remember the past to move towards the future. “Freedom then, freedom now means that we are still fighting now for equality, equity,” she said. In the city, people came together to honor the past and celebrate the present.

headlines.title: Fire breaks out at beached riverboat in Vallejo
headlines.description: Firefighters knocked out a fire at Grand Romance, a beached riverboat in Vallejo, according to authorities on Saturday. The fire department said the flames were burning at the top of the deck of the beached riverboat. The fire was extinguished by 1 a.m. Fire officials note this is the second fire aboard the ship since it beached in Vallejo in 2023.

headlines.title: Man arrested after hours-long standoff in Santa Rosa
headlines.description: A man was arrested following an hours-long standoff in Santa Rosa, according to police. Police said that, around 11 p.m., officers responded to a man who had climbed onto the roof of an RV on Santa Rosa Avenue and refused to come down. They say the man claimed he had a gun and dynamite, set the RV on fire, and threw large rocks. Police and firefighters were finally able to get him to come down around 1 a.m. Saturday, as the RV roof started to collapse. Investigators have only identified the suspect as a 47-year-old Santa Rosa man on pre-trial release in a criminal case.

headlines.title: Police looking for attempted kidnapping suspect in Hayward
headlines.description: Hayward police said they are looking for a suspect in an attempted kidnapping whom witnessed described as resembling a “Justin Bieber lookalike”. Police said officers were flagged down near Jackson Street and Soto Road on Friday at around 3:15 p.m. for an attempted kidnapping. Authorities said the female victim told officers she was walking north on Soto Road when she encountered a man walking south. As they passed each other, the man suddenly grabbed the victim and forcibly moved her about 15 to 20 feet, according to police. Officials said the victim screamed and struggled to break free until two people who witnessed the incident intervened, causing the suspect to release her and run away. Police said the suspect was last seen running on Soto Road and reached Jackson Street. Law enforcement described him as a white or Hispanic man, approximately 5 feet 7 inches tall, 120 pounds, with bushy hair, thin eyebrows and unique-colored eyes. According to officials, he was wearing a white T-shirt with red paint on it, black pants, and a black crossbody bag. Authorities added witnesses described the suspect as resembling a “Justin Bieber look-a-like”. The police department encourages anyone with information to contact their tip line at 510-293-7176.

headlines.title: World Cup crowds have San Jose businesses bringing in more staff and alcohol
headlines.description: San Pedro Square in downtown San Jose has continued to see enormous crowds day after day for World Cup watch parties. Thursday, the area was crowded with more than 30,000 fans clamoring to watch the Mexico versus South Korea matchup, so many people that security had to turn some away. The San Jose Earthquakes have designated the square the official Soccer Celebration headquarters through July 19, and fans seem to have taken to the watch party environment in the blocked-off downtown street. These watch parties began on July 11, and now businesses in the area have had more than a week to adjust to the massive influx of traffic. “It’s been absolutely insane, the Super Bowl on steroids,” said Douglas Cookerly, VP of Operations for the Sushi Confidential bar and restaurant in San Pedro Square. In particular, businesses say that drinks have been in demand — especially given the warm weather. They noted that the most in-demand drink is beer, followed closely by tequila. Cookerly said he was expecting big crowds during the World Cup, but didn’t anticipate the popularity of the watch parties to rise to this intense level. He said much like the World Cup is the chance for teams to shine on a global stage, this period of time is a chance for San Jose businesses to shine and make an impression on people who’ve never been there before. “We’ve been ordering beer and liquor every single day, doubling up on the food, calling in the reserve staff, yeah, it’s been all hands on deck,” he added. Sushi Confidential has been so busy that owner Randy Musterer posted on social media that the business got an alert from San Jose water about a possible water leak, but it turns out the business was just using a lot of water to wash all those dishes and flush toilets. Friday, fans filled San Pedro Square throughout the day to watch three major matches: USA versus Australia, Brazil versus Haiti, and Turkey versus Paraguay. San Jose resident Luke Van Poetsch said he went to the square for the community watch party experience. “I mean, here you get the atmosphere, you get the feels, you hear the vuvuzelas going off, it really adds to the atmosphere,” he said. The energy isn’t just drawing in locals; plenty of international visitors are stopping by as well. “It’s really, really good vibes,” said Cai Clarke, who is the bay area, some people say its kind of boring, but I’m here and its absolute vibes, so I’m having a great time.

headlines.title: Alameda County Fair kicks off, attracting thousands to the fairgrounds
headlines.description: The Alameda County Fair kicked off on Friday, and thousands of people did not waste time filling their fairgrounds. With nearly half a million people expected over the next few weeks, organizers say keeping everyone safe is just as important as keeping them entertained. Alameda County Sheriff’s deputies will be patrolling the fairgrounds every day, and fair leaders say they are working hand-in-hand with law enforcement to keep things running smoothly. “Very close relationship with Alameda County Sheriffs meetings everyday briefing, they can have a safe fun time,” Alameda County Spokesperson Liz Rosinski said. The fair had options to eat everywhere; from favorites like funnel cakes and turkey legs, to new creations like spam wonton tacos, the fair continues to find new ways to keep people coming back. “We’re so excited we wait for this all year. The start of the county fair is really the start of summer,” Rosinski said. The fair is also a big boost for the local economy, generating more than $100 million each year. Pete Suratos has the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: Security increases in downtown San Jose as thousands expected for World Cup watch party
headlines.description: Tens of thousands of soccer fans are expected to gather in San Pedro Square for the Türkiye-Paraguay World Cup match watch party, and organizers said they are keeping safety a top priority. Organizers celebrated the big turnout in downtown San Jose after more than 30,000 fans came through for Thursday’s Mexico-South Korea matchup. Organizers expect as many as 25,000 people to pass through downtown for Friday’s Türkiye-Paraguay World Cup match watch party. With large crowds, city leaders say public safety is just as important as the celebration. “It’s focusing on the public safety and how do we create a fun family environment at the same time,” Executive Director of San Jose Sports Authority John Poch said. To help do that, organizers have significantly increased security throughout downtown San Jose. “There are over 50 police officers here, let alone the 40 to 50 private security that we have mainly for crowd control and traffic control,” Poch said. The goal: keeping the watch parties family-friendly while making sure fans can enjoy safely. “I feel like it’s pretty safe and we brought the kids and it feels fun so, happy to be here!” Aaron Scholl, a fan, said. And the excitement continues to draw visitors from around the world. “The atmosphere is just pretty amazing right now, so I’m just excited to be here and represent Australia,” said Tom Prak, a fan visiting from Melbourne, Australia. As attendance continues to grow, organizers are reminding fans to arrive early, expect delays and have backup plans in place. “It’s really patience. Come here for the atmosphere, come here for the cultural celebration, but come early and have a Plan B,” Poch said. Organizers said they’re continuing to monitor crowd sizes throughout the day and will adjust staffing as needed.
